SEARCH
Package basicFiles.files.medias

Class Audio

java.lang.Object
basicFiles.files.BaseFile
basicFiles.files.medias.Audio
public class Audio extends BaseFile
Class representing an audio file (specifically in .wav format). This class provides functionality to synthesize sinusoidal sounds, manage an audio buffer, and handle playback using the Java Sound API.

Note: This component is currently in development.

Author:
Elias Kassas

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Audio(File folder, String fileName)
    Constructs an audio file within a specific folder.
    Audio(String fileName)
    Constructs an audio file in the current working directory.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    addFrequency(int freqHz, int durationMs)
    Synthesizes a sinusoidal wave at a specific frequency and adds it to the buffer.
    void
    addSilence(int durationMs)
    Adds a period of silence (zero amplitude) to the audio buffer.
    void
    append(Object... args)
    Appending data directly to the end of a WAVE file is not supported due to header requirements.
    boolean
    deletePhysicalFile()
    Physically deletes the file from the filesystem and flushes the playback clip.
    ByteArrayOutputStream
    getBufferGlobal()
    Retrieves the global audio buffer.
    void
    play()
    Starts playing the loaded audio from the beginning.
    void
    read()
    Reads the audio file from disk and initializes the playback Clip.
    void
    readPlayWait()
    Reads the file, starts playback, and blocks the current thread until the audio finishes.
    void
    setBufferGlobal(ByteArrayOutputStream bufferGlobal)
    Replaces the current audio buffer with a new one.
    void
    stop()
    Stops the current playback if the audio is running.
    void
    write(Object... args)
    Exports the accumulated buffer data into a physical WAVE file on disk.

    Methods inherited from class basicFiles.files.BaseFile

    exists, getFilePath, getName, rename, save, toString

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    equals, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • Audio

      public Audio(File folder, String fileName)
      Constructs an audio file within a specific folder.
      Parameters:
      folder - The parent directory.
      fileName - The name of the file (extension .wav is applied automatically).

    • Audio

      public Audio(String fileName)
      Constructs an audio file in the current working directory.
      Parameters:
      fileName - The name of the file.

  • Method Details

    • addFrequency

      public void addFrequency(int freqHz, int durationMs)
      Synthesizes a sinusoidal wave at a specific frequency and adds it to the buffer.
      Parameters:
      freqHz - The frequency in Hertz (Hz).
      durationMs - The duration of the sound in milliseconds (ms).

    • addSilence

      public void addSilence(int durationMs)
      Adds a period of silence (zero amplitude) to the audio buffer.
      Parameters:
      durationMs - The duration of the silence in milliseconds (ms).

    • write

      public void write(Object... args)
      Exports the accumulated buffer data into a physical WAVE file on disk.
      Specified by:
      write in class BaseFile
      Parameters:
      args - Ignored.

    • read

      public void read()
      Reads the audio file from disk and initializes the playback Clip.
      Specified by:
      read in class BaseFile

    • play

      public void play()
      Starts playing the loaded audio from the beginning.

    • stop

      public void stop()
      Stops the current playback if the audio is running.

    • readPlayWait

      public void readPlayWait()
      Reads the file, starts playback, and blocks the current thread until the audio finishes.

    • deletePhysicalFile

      public boolean deletePhysicalFile()
      Physically deletes the file from the filesystem and flushes the playback clip.
      Specified by:
      deletePhysicalFile in class BaseFile
      Returns:
      true if successfully deleted; false otherwise.

    • getBufferGlobal

      public ByteArrayOutputStream getBufferGlobal()
      Retrieves the global audio buffer.
      Returns:
      The current ByteArrayOutputStream.

    • setBufferGlobal

      public void setBufferGlobal(ByteArrayOutputStream bufferGlobal)
      Replaces the current audio buffer with a new one.
      Parameters:
      bufferGlobal - The new audio buffer.

    • append

      public void append(Object... args) throws Exception
      Appending data directly to the end of a WAVE file is not supported due to header requirements.
      Specified by:
      append in class BaseFile
      Parameters:
      args - Variable arguments used by subclasses for specialized appending.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - Always thrown.
      Exception - If an error occurs during the append operation.
